Fred Frith holds the title of Professor Emeritus of Music at Mills College in Oakland, California, where he taught composition, contemporary performance, and improvisation until his retirement in 2018[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fred_Frith), [2](https://www.fredfrith.com/this-and-that), [3](https://www.forcedexposure.com/Artists/FRITH.FRED.html).

Beyond his emeritus status at Mills College, Frith is also involved in other academic institutions. He co-directs an improvisation master's program at the Musik Akademie Basel in Switzerland[4](https://grokipedia.com/page/Fred_Frith), [5](https://kalx.berkeley.edu/fred-frith-interview/), [6](https://www.fredfrith.com/bla-bla-bio), [3](https://www.forcedexposure.com/Artists/FRITH.FRED.html). Additionally, he serves as visiting faculty at the Universidad Austral in Valdivia, Chile, where he is contributing to the establishment of a new School of Music and Sound Art[3](https://www.forcedexposure.com/Artists/FRITH.FRED.html).

Frith remains an active musician, performing solo and with various groups, including Normal (a duo with Sudhu Tewari) and Frelonia (with Lotte Anker and Núria Andorrà).[5](https://kalx.berkeley.edu/fred-frith-interview/), [6](https://www.fredfrith.com/bla-bla-bio)
